question: How do prices differ across the three furnishing status categories: furnished, semi-furnished, and unfurnished?

analysis.design_rationale: A violinplot combined with a jittered scatter overlay offers a deep dive into the price distribution 'shape' for each furnishingstatus, visualizing both density and individual datapoints. Violinplots reveal multimodality or skew that boxplots may obscure, while the overlay helps spot clustering, dispersion, and even possible price gaps for each furnishing status. This hybrid approach is powerful where outliers or distributional nuances matter for actionable insights in pricing.
analysis.target_insight: Not only median and range, but also the fine structure of the price distribution (e.g. bimodality, skew), plus granular visibility into how prices scatter within each furnishing status group, showing whether semi-furnished units have tighter or wider price spreads or whether any category shows potential clustering or uniformity.

chart_encodings.color: Violin fill colors by furnishingstatus; semi-transparent scatter points for price
chart_encodings.size: Scatter marker size uniform; no additional encoding
chart_encodings.other_aesthetics: Alpha blending for scatter (to de-emphasize overplotting), violin edge width

transformations: Map df['furnishingstatus'] to categorical codes for plotting axes
Split df into three subgroups by furnishingstatus, each providing df['price'] for violin kernel density estimation
Randomly jitter x axis positions for scatter overlay within each category to reduce overlap
data_preparation_steps: Read the CSV file 'csv_c_squared\206.csv' and assign to df
For each category, filter corresponding df['price'] values, determine x position for scatter jittering
suggestion.matplotlib_code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

df = pd.read_csv("csv_c_squared\206.csv")
categories = ["furnished", "semi-furnished", "unfurnished"]
positions = np.arange(1, 4)
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 5))
violin_data = [df[df["furnishingstatus"] == cat]["price"] for cat in categories]
violin = ax.violinplot(violin_data, positions=positions, widths=0.6, showmeans=False, showmedians=True, showextrema=False)
colors = ["#4577b7", "#42b360", "#e3802f"]
for i, body in enumerate(violin["bodies"]):
    body.set_facecolor(colors[i])
    body.set_alpha(0.45)
    body.set_edgecolor("#222222")
    body.set_linewidth(1.3)
for i, cat in enumerate(categories):
    data_y = df[df["furnishingstatus"] == cat]["price"].values
    data_x = np.random.normal(loc=positions[i], scale=0.07, size=len(data_y))
    ax.scatter(data_x, data_y, s=16, color=colors[i], alpha=0.28, edgecolor="none")
ax.set_xticks(positions)
ax.set_xticklabels(categories)
ax.set_ylabel("Price", fontsize=13)
ax.set_xlabel("Furnishing Status", fontsize=13)
ax.set_title("Full Price Distribution by Furnishing Status\n(Violinplot with Individual Jittered Prices)", fontsize=14)
ax.grid(axis="y", linestyle=":", alpha=0.6)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
